findings.title: requirements.txt lists packages with no version pins
findings.quote:
pandas
scikit-learn
numpy
matplotlib
torch
fvcore
einops
thop
timm
reformer_pytorch
openpyxl
seaborn
pywt
findings.claim: All 13 dependencies are listed without any version constraints, and no environment lockfile (conda env / pip freeze) is provided.
findings.concern: Exact reproduction of the reported MSE/MAE can drift with library versions (numpy/torch/sklearn), and the environment cannot be rebuilt deterministically.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide pinned versions (e.g. torch==x.y, numpy==x.y) or an environment.yml / pip-freeze used for the reported runs.

findings.title: Several headline benchmarks (ETT/ECL/Traffic/Solar/PEMS/Exchange) and their Q-files not in repo
findings.quote:
2. Some datasets can be obtained from [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1l51QsKvQPcqILT3DwfjCgx8Dsg2rpjot/view?usp=drive_link) or [Tsinghua Cloud](https://cloud.tsinghua.edu.cn/f/2ea5ca3d621e4e5ba36a/).  The others can be obtained from the links in Appendix B. 
Some datasets are already in the folder ```./dataset```. 
The python script ```Generate_corrmat.ipynb``` for generating Pearson correlation matrices and Q matrices is also provided in ```./dataset```.
findings.claim: The bundled dataset/ contains only cars, covid, DowJones, ILI, nasdaq, power, SP500, unemployment, weather, website, wiki. Scripts reference ./dataset/ETT-small/, ./dataset/electricity/, ./dataset/traffic/, ./dataset/Solar/, ./dataset/PEMS/, ./dataset/exchange_rate/, ./dataset/METR_LA/, none of which (nor their *_ratioX.npy Q-files) exist in the repo.
findings.concern: Reproducing the long-term-forecasting headline tables for the standard benchmarks requires downloading data and re-running the notebook to regenerate the Q matrices; the repo is not self-contained for those rows.
findings.resolution: Authors: bundle (or give a direct, resolvable archive of) the standard-benchmark CSVs plus their precomputed Q/channel .npy files, as is done for the novel datasets.

findings.title: Learnable bias delta1/delta2 added inside OrthoTrans, not in paper's equation
findings.claim: The input transform (Q_i^T x, line 105) adds a learnable bias delta1 (shape [1,enc_in,1,seq_len]); the output reconstruction adds delta2 the same way at OLinear.py:118 ('x = torch.einsum(...) + self.delta2'). delta1/delta2 are nn.Parameters declared at OLinear.py:80-81.
findings.concern: The paper presents OrthoTrans (§4.2) as a pure orthogonal projection x -> Q_i^T x and reconstruction with Q_o, with no additive learnable term; the code's transform is therefore not exactly the published map, a result-affecting component the paper does not mention.
findings.resolution: Authors: document delta1/delta2 in the method description (or in an ablation), or confirm they are negligible (initialized to zero) and report the effect of removing them.
findings.paper_ref: Section 4.2 OrthoTrans, equations around the QiΛQi^T decomposition

findings.title: Shipped scripts use 30 epochs / patience 5; paper states 50 epochs / patience 10
findings.claim: Most OLinear scripts set --train_epochs 30 and --patience 5 (counts across scripts/: 23 use 30 epochs, 5 use 50; 73 use patience 5, 31 use patience 10).
findings.concern: Appendix D states 'Training is performed for up to 50 epochs with early stopping ... if the validation performance does not improve for 10 consecutive epochs', which the released configs do not match; reported numbers may come from a different schedule than documented.
findings.resolution: Authors: reconcile the per-dataset script schedules with the App. D description, or note that the schedule was tuned per dataset.

findings.title: Reported metric is min over test-batch-sizes and decoder stages, selected by test MSE
findings.quote:
mse_plus_mae = [a + b for a, b in zip(mse_list, mae_list)]

# print min_mse
# print(mse_list, mae_list)
min_index = mse_plus_mae.index(min(mse_plus_mae))
mse = mse_list[min_index]
mae = mae_list[min_index]
findings.claim: test() builds mse_list/mae_list over intermediate decoder stages (when the model returns a tuple and --seq_inter=1, default) and reports the stage with minimum test MSE+MAE; run.py:566-576 additionally evaluates every entry of test_batch_size_list and keeps the test batch size with the lowest test MSE.
findings.concern: Both mechanisms select the reported test metric using the test set itself; if active they constitute selection-on-test (a mild optimistic bias).
findings.resolution: Confirm these selections are inert for the headline runs (see note) and remove/disable the test-driven argmin, or report a fixed stage / fixed test batch size chosen without the test set.
